AuditEventOut.description: Public audit event shape, chain hashes excluded.
AuditEventOut.title: AuditEventOut

BatchScrapeRequest.description:
Request body for POST /v1/scrape/batch.

Accepts 1–20 URLs and a concurrency cap 1–10. All other fields mirror
the shared scrape params from ScrapeBody and are applied uniformly to
every URL in the batch.
BatchScrapeRequest.title: BatchScrapeRequest

ttl_seconds.description: New TTL in seconds (30s. 24h). Omit for the store default.
ttl_seconds.title: Ttl Seconds

ExtractRequest.description:
POST /v1/extract request body, schema-driven extraction (deterministic) + bring-your-own-model extraction.

Two extraction modes:
- Deterministic (default, model_provider absent): CSS/regex/keyword extraction. Requires 'schema'. Returns {"url","data","token_estimate"}.
- Bring-your-own-model (model_provider present): GhostCrawl fetches and prepares clean Markdown; your connected model performs the semantic extraction (you are not billed for model inference here, only the request quota). Returns {"urls","data","token_estimate"}.

Security notes:
- The 'schema' field is validated before any fetch (fail-fast input guard).
- URLs are validated against private, loopback, and metadata targets before any fetch.
- Error responses never echo the schema body, fetched content, or model credentials.
- model_provider.api_key is request-scoped only: never persisted, logged, or returned in any response body or error message.

behavior_actions.description: BYO behavior: trusted-input actions replayed on the loaded page instead of the built-in human session. Sanitized + bounded server-side (max 256 actions). Available on every tier.
behavior_actions.title: Behavior Actions

engine.description: Engine override: auto (default) | chrome | firefox | webkit.
engine.title: Engine

identity_country.description: Optional two-letter country code to pin (geo-filters the exit and derives timezone; tier-gated). Omitted = timezone follows the exit.
identity_country.title: Identity Country

language.description: Optional browser language tag (e.g. 'en-US','es-ES'). Sets the identity's locale and language headers coherently, independent of the exit country. Available on every tier.
language.title: Language

model_provider.description: BYO model credentials dict: {type, api_key, model, base_url?}. When present → BYO-LLM extraction path (GhostCrawl bills the request, not the inference tokens, inference runs on the user's connected model). When absent → deterministic CSS/regex/keyword extraction (default).
model_provider.title: Model Provider

prompt.description: Free-form extraction instruction for BYO-LLM mode. Allowed without a schema (schema-less prose extraction). Ignored in deterministic mode (model_provider absent).
prompt.title: Prompt

schema.description: JSON Schema describing the desired extraction output. Required for deterministic extraction (model_provider absent). Optional for BYO-LLM extraction (model_provider present), when provided, the LLM is instructed to respond in JSON-mode conforming to this schema.
schema.title: Schema

url.description: URL to fetch and extract from (single-URL mode). Required when 'urls' is not provided.
url.title: Url

urls.description: Multi-URL cross-page aggregation (bring-your-own-model mode only). Each URL is fetched and converted to clean Markdown, then concatenated with per-URL delimiter headers; one aggregated model call extracts across all pages. Bounded to ≤10 URLs. Cannot be combined with 'url' when both are provided.
urls.title: Urls

ExtractRequest.title: ExtractRequest

IdentityPayload.description: Encrypted identity payload returned to the caller.
IdentityPayload.title: IdentityPayload

IdentityRequest.description:
Request body for POST /v1/identity.

extension (single-endpoint-with-source):
source="user" → BYO local path; carries the full local identity payload.
source="gc_identity" → managed cloud identity (paid plan).
source=None → Legacy path (operating system and browser required).

For legacy path with mobile operating system values (ios, android): device_model is required.
For legacy path with desktop operating system values (macos, windows, linux): viewport is required.
IdentityRequest.title: IdentityRequest

MapBody.description:
Firecrawl-compatible /v1/map request body.

naming convention warnings for intentional camelCase).
MapBody.title: MapBody

ProblemDetails.description: RFC-9457 problem+json error body. Returned as application/problem+json for validation (422) and other error responses.

detail.description: Human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence.

instance.description: Request id correlating this response to server logs.

retryable.description: Whether retrying the same request may succeed.

ProblemDetails.title: ProblemDetails

ProfileCreateRequest.description: POST /v1/profiles body, create a named persistent profile.

identity_spec.description: Public IdentityRequest spec (operating system, browser, device, viewport, locale, and timezone). The profile mints a STABLE deterministic identity_id from it, so every scrape with profile=<name> rehydrates the SAME fingerprint. When omitted, a default desktop-chrome identity is minted.
identity_spec.title: Identity Spec

name.description: Profile name. Unique per org (409 on conflict).

RotateSecretResponse.description:
Response for POST /v1/webhooks/{id}/rotate-secret.

`secret` is the new signing secret. The previous secret remains valid
for a short grace period so in-flight deliveries can still be verified.
`prev_secret_expires_at` is when the grace period ends.

prev_secret_expires_at.description: When the previous signing secret expires and the grace period ends.
prev_secret_expires_at.title: Prev Secret Expires At

secret.description: New signing secret. The previous secret remains valid during the grace period.
secret.title: Secret

monitor_mode.description: When True, fires notify_webhook only when content hash changes.
monitor_mode.title: Monitor Mode

name.description: Schedule name. Unique per org (409 on conflict).
name.title: Name

notify_webhook.description: HTTPS webhook URL for change-monitor notifications.
notify_webhook.title: Notify Webhook

ScrapePageResult.description:
One per-URL scrape result inside ScrapeResult.results.

``extra="allow"`` because the fleet/ attaches additional diagnostic
members (headers, resolved_url, identity_id, result_error,.) that vary by
engine + path; the documented core is what every SDK reads.

result_error.description: Per-item TARGET error {code, retryable} when the fetch failed.
result_error.title: Result Error

status_code.description: Upstream HTTP status of the fetch.
status_code.title: Status Code

ScrapePageResult.title: ScrapePageResult

batch_identity_mode.description: "persist" (default): same identity for all URLs in a batch. "randomize": fresh identity per URL (each counts against quota).
batch_identity_mode.title: Batch Identity Mode

behavior_actions.description: BYO behavior: a list of trusted-input actions replayed on the loaded page instead of the built-in human session. Each item is {"kind":"move","x":int,"y":int} | {"kind":"wheel","x":int,"y":int,"dy":int} | {"kind":"dwell","ms":int}. Sanitized + bounded server-side (max 256 actions). Available on every tier.
behavior_actions.title: Behavior Actions

chunk_tokens.description: Maximum tokens per markdown chunk. Clamped server-side to [256, 32768] regardless of caller input.
chunk_tokens.title: Chunk Tokens

engine.description: Engine override. 'auto' (default): the identity's fingerprint decides the engine. 'chrome'|'firefox'|'webkit': force that engine, enforced only if coherent with the identity (e.g. iOS identities are always webkit); incoherent picks are rejected, never coerced to a hybrid.
engine.title: Engine

format.description: Response format. 'html' (default): the fully rendered DOM. 'markdown': returns a {markdown, chunks, citations, token_estimate} envelope with prompt-injection-neutralized, token-budgeted output. 'pdf': returns application/pdf bytes (Chrome identities only; Firefox and WebKit return 400 pdf_engine_unsupported).
format.title: Format

full_page.description: When screenshot=true, capture the FULL document height instead of just the visible viewport. Ignored when screenshot_selector is set.
full_page.title: Full Page

identity_country.description: Optional two-letter country code (e.g. 'US','DE'). When set, the exit is geo-filtered to that country and the identity's timezone is derived from it. When omitted, the timezone follows the rotating exit country. Language is independent, see the `language` field.
identity_country.title: Identity Country

identity_spec.description: Inline identity specification. Creates an ephemeral identity.
identity_spec.title: Identity Spec

include_citations.description: When format=markdown, include deduplicated citation list {url, anchor_text} from the scraped page.
include_citations.title: Include Citations

language.description: Optional browser language tag (e.g. 'en-US','es-ES','de-DE'). Sets the identity's locale and language headers coherently, independent of the exit country. When omitted, the identity's default locale is used. Available on every tier.
language.title: Language

profile.description: Named persistent profile (multi-accounting). Resolves to a durable identity_id (→ same deterministic fingerprint) + optional saved storage_state (→ cookies/localStorage) for the calling org. Reuses the exact identity+cookies on every request, with no 30-minute sticky-session expiry. 404 if the named profile does not exist for the org.
profile.title: Profile

proxy.description: Bring-your-own proxy URL. Schemes: socks5, socks5h, http, https. Any credentials in the URL are redacted from logs. Example: socks5://user:pass@host:1080.
proxy.title: Proxy

screenshot.description: When true, a screenshot is captured during the fetch and returned as a base64-encoded PNG in result['screenshot']. Off by default.
screenshot.title: Screenshot

screenshot_selector.description: When screenshot=true, clip the capture to the first DOM element matching this CSS selector (element screenshot). Takes precedence over full_page.
screenshot_selector.title: Screenshot Selector

session.description: Sticky-session token (paid tiers). Requests sharing a token reuse the same identity → same exit + cookies (returning-visitor coherence). Bounded TTL; opaque [A-Za-z0-9._-], max 128 chars.
session.title: Session

stream.description: When true, returns text/event-stream with one SSE event per chunk. Per-tenant concurrency is capped (default 5). Final 'done' event carries estimated_llm_input_tokens.
stream.title: Stream

ScrapeResult.description:
POST /v1/scrape 200 response body.

Documents the customer-facing shape so the OpenAPI 200 schema is no longer an
empty ``{}`` (Kiota/typed SDKs need a named schema). ``extra="allow"`` keeps
it forward-compatible with the markdown envelope (``format``/``markdown``/
``chunks``/``citations``/``token_estimate``/``estimated_llm_input_tokens``)
and the html envelope (``results``/``identity_id``/``identity_ids``/
``identities_used``) without over-constraining the route (which returns a
JSONResponse, so this model documents but does not coerce the body).

format.description: Output format when a non-default format was requested.
format.title: Format

status.description: Aggregate run status (e.g. completed / partial / failed).
status.title: Status

token_estimate.description: Estimated token count of the markdown output.
token_estimate.title: Token Estimate

ScrapeResult.title: ScrapeResult

country.description: Optional 2-letter ISO country code for geo-filter. Tavily v1 ignores this silently.

engine.description: Search engine to use: 'brave', 'tavily', 'google', 'bing', or 'yandex'. May also be passed via X-GhostCrawl-Search-Engine header.

freshness.description: Optional freshness filter (adapter-specific; Brave: 'pd'/'pw'/'pm'/'py' for day/week/month/year).

vertical.description: Engine-specific vertical (google only): 'search' (default) | 'jobs' | 'scholar' | 'play' | 'reviews'. Ignored by brave/tavily.

profile.description: Named persistent profile (gologin parity). Rehydrates the saved identity bundle for this session, same deterministic fingerprint as /v1/scrape with profile=<name>.
profile.title: Profile

profile_id.title: Profile Id

allow_private_targets.description: Allow private/internal IP targets (hosted mode only). Requires webhooks:admin scope when deployment_mode=hosted.
allow_private_targets.title: Allow Private Targets

event_types.description: Event types to subscribe to. Must be non-empty subset of: ['agent.run.completed', 'agent.run.failed', 'budget.exceeded', 'budget.warning', 'cost.daily.threshold_critical', 'cost.daily.threshold_warning', 'cost.month_end.projected_overrun', 'crawl.completed', 'dataset.updated', 'extract.completed', 'extract.failed', 'manual_test', 'recording.saved', 'scrape.completed', 'scrape.failed', 'session.ended', 'team.api_key.created', 'team.api_key.revoked', 'team.signup.verified'].

get.description:
retrieve the effective policy for a scope.

Requires budgets:read scope for your own scope, or budgets:admin for
cross-organisation access. Cross-tenant or missing-id requests return 404
(not 403) to prevent enumeration.

Query params:
scope_type: "user" | "org" | "session" (default "org")
scope_id: UUID string; defaults to caller's org_id when scope_type=org,
or caller's user_id when scope_type=user.

put.description:
upsert a budget policy row.

Auth: budgets:write for own scope; budgets:admin for cross-tenant scope.

before any DB write (fast-fail).

Audit: emits budget.policy_created (INSERT) or budget.policy_updated (UPDATE), the store's upsert re-reads the row post-conflict to detect which happened
(compare created_at == updated_at). Best-effort; never raises to caller.

delete.description:
remove a policy row.

Returns 204 on success. Existing usage counters are preserved, historical
usage data is never deleted when a policy is removed.

Cross-tenant or missing → 404 budget_not_found (not 403; enumeration guard).

Audit: emits budget.policy_deleted on success.

get.description:
live budget counter snapshot.

Returns {tokens_used, tokens_remaining, calls_used, calls_remaining,
crawl_pages_used, crawl_pages_remaining, scope_type, scope_id,
source: "live"}.

*_remaining = max(0, limit - used) when the policy has a limit, else null
(unlimited). Matches the shape emitted by the SSE budget-stream endpoint.

Cross-tenant → 404 budget_not_found (not 403; enumeration guard).

post.description:
Engine-agnostic live-view frame capture (cross-engine).

Returns a single base64-encoded JPEG frame of a live interactive session,
using the same capture path across Chromium, Firefox, and WebKit.

Gating:
1. Tenant authentication
2. Revocation check
3. Tier gate (paid plan required, free denied, pro+/trial allowed), checked before ownership
4. Session ownership (the session must belong to the authenticated organisation)

Returns 200 {"format":"jpeg","data":"<base64>","engine","width","height"} on success;
403 cdp_not_enabled (tier) / cdp_revoked; 404 session_not_found; 502 frame_failed.

Mint a short-TTL signed URL for cloud CDP access.

Returns 200 {"url": "wss://.", "expires_in_seconds": 120} on success.

schema.title: Response Submit Contact V1 Contact Post

get.description:
cursor pagination.
{items, next_cursor, total_count}. This is the one explicit breaking shape
change in; no SDK consumers shipped against the old shape. owner_id requires crawl:admin scope to be honored. created_at_from > created_at_to → 400 (not 422).
Error envelope for 403 unchanged (at error layer).

schema.description: Idempotency key (8–256 ASCII printable chars). Supply a fresh UUID or ULID per logically-distinct request. Replaying the same request with the same key returns the cached response without re-execution. Same key + different body → 409 idempotency_key_reused_with_different_body. Body > 1 MiB with this header → 413.

Replaying the same request with the same key returns the cached response without re-execution. Same key + different body → 409 idempotency_key_reused_with_different_body. Body > 1 MiB with this header → 413.

Idempotency key (8–256 ASCII printable chars). Supply a fresh UUID or ULID per logically-distinct request. Replaying the same request with the same key returns the cached response without re-execution. Same key + different body → 409 idempotency_key_reused_with_different_body. Body > 1 MiB with this header → 413.

queue an async deep crawl.

Returns immediately with run_id and status="queued".
Webhook fires on completion when webhook_url is provided.
Auth: crawl:write scope required.
Billing: crawl_pages_delta (Option A).

schema.description: Idempotency key (8–256 ASCII printable chars). Supply a fresh UUID or ULID per logically-distinct request. Replaying the same request with the same key returns the cached response without re-execution. Same key + different body → 409 idempotency_key_reused_with_different_body. Body > 1 MiB with this header → 413.

poll deep crawl job status.

Returns current status and result count. Results array included when
status = "completed".

Auth: crawl:read scope required.

cursor pagination.
(created_at, id) base64 tuple; response gains total_count (additive). owner_id requires dataset:admin scope. created_at_from > created_at_to → 400 (not 422).
preserved: dataset.listed audit row still emitted.

post.description:
register a new dataset sink.

Requires scope dataset:write. Credentials are stored encrypted and are
never returned in the response. Duplicate names return 400 dataset_name_conflict.
Success returns 201 with dataset metadata.

delete.description:
delete.

Returns 200 on success, 404 on miss.
Emits dataset.deleted audit.

get.description:
metadata only.

Cross-tenant miss returns dataset_not_found (404, not 403; enumeration guard).
Credentials are never included in the response.

paginated rows from a dataset.

Cross-tenant or missing → 404 dataset_not_found (not 403; enumeration guard).
Returns {rows: [{row_id, written_at, payload}], next_cursor}.

post.description:
append rows to an existing dataset.

Cross-tenant or missing dataset → 404 dataset_not_found (not 403; enumeration guard).
Returns {appended: N} on success.
Rejects when rows is not a list of objects (422) or exceeds the per-request cap (413).
post.summary: Append rows to a dataset (landing zone)

get.description:
SDK service-discovery endpoint.

Returns available component endpoints + load hints (DI).
Response is cached in-process for CACHE_TTL seconds.

Requires Authorization: Bearer <token>.

post.description: Return a license-bound short-TTL pre-signed engine manifest.

schema.description: Override body's platform value
schema.title: Platform

schema.description: Optional body: {platform, channel, ttl_seconds}. Defaults: platform from User-Agent if recognizable, channel='stable', ttl_seconds=900.
schema.title: Body

post.description:
structured extraction (deterministic default) + bring-your-own-model extraction.

Two modes (selected by model_provider):

DETERMINISTIC (model_provider absent, default):
1. The schema is validated (input guard).
2. The URL is validated against private/loopback/metadata targets.
3. The page is fetched and rendered.
4. Structured data is extracted via CSS/regex/keyword rules.
5. The result is validated against the schema, 422 on mismatch (field path only).
Returns {"url","data","token_estimate"}.

BRING-YOUR-OWN-MODEL (model_provider present):
1. Target URLs are resolved (urls or [url]).
2. The schema is validated if present.
3. At least one of prompt or schema is required, 400 prompt_or_schema_required otherwise.
4. Model credentials are validated, 400 invalid_model_provider on error (the message never echoes the key).
5. Every target URL is validated before fetching, 400 ssrf_blocked.
6. Each URL is fetched and converted to clean Markdown.
7. Your connected model performs the extraction.
8. With a schema present, the result is validated, 422 on mismatch.
Returns {"urls","data","token_estimate"}.

Security:
- 401 without a valid Bearer token.
- model_provider.api_key is request-scoped: never persisted, logged, or echoed.
- Error responses never echo the schema body, fetched content, or credentials.

schema.description: Idempotency key (8–256 ASCII printable chars). Supply a fresh UUID or ULID per logically-distinct request. Replaying the same request with the same key returns the cached response without re-execution. Same key + different body → 409 idempotency_key_reused_with_different_body. Body > 1 MiB with this header → 413.

schema.title: Response Saas Extract V1 Extract Post

post.description:
Create an encrypted identity payload.

requires Authorization: Bearer <token>.

1.
2.
On HIT: return encrypted blob.
3.

Encrypts the resulting
identity configuration, and returns an opaque encrypted envelope.

The plaintext identity configuration is NEVER returned in the response.

delete.description:
remove a KV entry.

Returns {deleted: true}. 404 on miss). 403 without kv:write.

get.description:
read a KV entry.

Returns {key, value, value_type}. For blobs, returns bytes with
application/octet-stream content-type. 404 on miss).

post.description:
sitemap URL discovery.

1. The seed URL is validated against private/loopback/metadata targets.
2. The request is quota-checked.
3. URLs are discovered from the site's sitemaps.
4. Results are filtered to the same registrable domain by default.
5. An optional case-insensitive substring filter is applied to URLs.
6. Results are capped at 10000 regardless of the requested limit.

get.description: Return current user profile + primary team.

GDPR self-service data export.

Returns a JSON bundle of the caller's org data: dataset rows, KV entries,
session summaries, API key prefixes (NEVER full keys), audit log rows, webhook
subscriptions.
org_id clauses prevent any other tenant's data from leaking.

Bundle schema:
{
"org_id": "<uuid>",
"exported_at": "<iso8601>",
"datasets": [.],
"kv_entries": [.],
"sessions": [.],
"api_keys": [.], # prefixes only (never plaintext)
"audit_log": [.], # most-recent 500 rows
"webhooks": [.],
}

schema.title: Response Export Account V1 Me Export Get

get.description: Return cost/usage report scoped strictly to caller's primary_team_id.

schema.title: Response Get Usage V1 Me Usage Get

post.description:
Return ``page.content`` for the live session, capped at 5 MB.

Over-cap returns ``truncated=True`` + ``dom=None`` (RESEARCH §Pattern 7);
callers retry with finer scope or fall back to /v1/page/eval for
selector-rooted extraction.

Run caller-supplied JavaScript in the pinned page context.

All execution paths (disabled, timeout-killed, result-capped, PII-filtered)
are audit-logged. A pre-execution audit record is written before the watchdog
fires so interrupted evaluations remain traceable, paired with a final
success/failure/denied record sharing the same ``eval_id`` correlation key.

The result payload is never written to the audit log, only its size and hash.
post.summary: Evaluate JavaScript on the pinned page

post.description:
Return the HAR-equivalent JSON for the live session.

Captures all network traffic recorded during the session and returns it
in HAR format. Responses over 5 MB return a truncation flag in the body
rather than an error.
post.summary: Capture HAR for the live session
Upload a file to a file input

get.description: Public pricing catalog. No authentication required.

cursor pagination. new endpoint. owner_id requires profile:admin to be honored; silently
ignored without it (not 403). created_at_from > created_at_to → 400 (not 422). tier gate NOT applied at list layer.

post.description:
mint a durable {org, name} → identity binding.

409 on duplicate name; 422 on invalid identity_spec.

delete.description: remove a named binding. 204 on success, 404 on miss.

get.description: fetch a named binding (404 if absent for org).

rename and/or rebind storage_state.

The identity_id is IMMUTABLE (changing it would silently change the fingerprint
and break multi-accounting coherence). 404 if absent; 409 on rename collision.
put.summary: Rename a profile and/or rebind its storage_state

get.description:
curated proxy provider list.

Bearer-gated. Returns the operator-curated list of proxy
providers with referral URLs (carrying the operator's creator code) and
pricing summaries. The customer signs up here, pays the provider, and brings
the creds.

Security:, referral URLs are not secrets, but the customer-facing
list is bearer-gated (a 140. 4-era extract-method refactor accidentally
promoted the unauthenticated renderer to the route + orphaned this gated handler;
restored 2026-06-11).

read up to N items from the queue.

Returns {items: [{id, url, meta}]}. Empty list when nothing available.

post.summary: Push URLs into a persistent queue

snapshot queue metrics.

Returns {length, acked, pending}. All counts are for this org's queue only

cursor pagination. response gains total_count (additive); items[*] shape unchanged. owner_id filter requires recordings:admin scope to be honored;
without it, the filter is silently dropped (not 403). created_at_from > created_at_to → 400 (not 422). tier gate NOT applied at list layer.

delete.description:
DELETE /v1/recordings/{recording_id}.

Scope: recordings:write. Returns 204 on success, 404 on miss/cross-tenant.
Emits recording.deleted audit.

get.description:
404 byte-identical for missing AND cross-tenant.

enumeration guard: NEVER 403 for cross-tenant by-id reads.

Get the HLS-style manifest pointer for a visual recording.

Returns 200 {manifest_url, frame_count, status, engine} or 404 for
cross-tenant/missing (byte-identical, /91 pattern).

List paginated frame references for frame-by-frame replay.

Returns 200 {frames: [str], next_cursor: int} or 404 for cross-tenant/missing.
next_cursor == -1 means no more pages.

schema.description: Start frame index (cursor-paginated)
schema.title: Cursor

schema.description: Max frames per page
schema.title: Limit

post.description:
Start a visual recording for a live session.

Creates a session_recordings row.
Returns 201 with the recording row id.

Security gates (in order):
1. scope gate: recordings:write
2. tier gate: org.recording_enabled
3. ownership gate: session ownership verified

Stop a visual recording and finalize (assemble m3u8 manifest).

Returns 200 with manifest_ref.

Exchange API license for a short-lived container image pull token.
Returns: JSON with ``token`` (signed token) and ``expires_in`` (3600).

get.description: list.

post.description:
create a cron-triggered scrape/crawl schedule.

Validations:
- schedule:write scope required (403 forbidden_scope on miss)
- cron_expr must parse (422 invalid_cron)
- job_type must be 'scrape', 'crawl', or 'change_monitor' (422 invalid_job_type)
- notify_webhook must be SSRF-safe HTTPS URL if provided (422 invalid_webhook)
- per-org count < cap (429 schedule_cap_reached)
- unique name per org (409 schedule_exists)

get.description: 404 on miss/cross-tenant.

synthetic run history from last_run_at.

Returns a list of run summary objects derived from the schedule's stored
last_run_at + last_result_hash. A schedule that has never fired returns an
empty list. One that has fired returns a single summary with status='completed'.

Status values: 'completed' (ran and produced a hash), 'pending' (never ran).
This endpoint allows callers to poll for schedule execution without a separate
run-history table, the writes last_run_at on every successful fire.
get.summary: List run history for a schedule

post.description:
Bearer-gated scrape with identity field.

Accepts:
- ``identity: "id_."`` Re-use a persisted identity (no quota charge).
- ``identity_spec: {.}`` Inline identity creation (counts against quota).
- neither Host-identity fallback; warns in response.
- Both → 422 invalid_request.

is unreachable from this route, profile.identity_id is always populated. stream=True branch returns text/event-stream with per-tenant cap.

schema.description: Idempotency key (8–256 ASCII printable chars). Supply a fresh UUID or ULID per logically-distinct request. Replaying the same request with the same key returns the cached response without re-execution. Same key + different body → 409 idempotency_key_reused_with_different_body. Body > 1 MiB with this header → 413.

Gc-Initial-Status-Code.description: Upstream HTTP status of the initial fetch (string-encoded integer).

Gc-Resolved-Url.description: Final URL after redirects; falls back to the requested URL.

post.description:
concurrent URL scrape (max 20 URLs, max concurrency 10).

processes each URL with authentication. Each URL is processed
(batch_identity_mode="persist" → one identity is created and reused for every URL).

DoS mitigations (/02):

schema.description: Idempotency key (8–256 ASCII printable chars). Supply a fresh UUID or ULID per logically-distinct request. Replaying the same request with the same key returns the cached response without re-execution. Same key + different body → 409 idempotency_key_reused_with_different_body. Body > 1 MiB with this header → 413.

get.description:
Fetch a screenshot blob by UUID ref, owner-scoped.
Returns: 200 ``{ref, png_base64, page_url_host, created_at}`` on success.
404 ``{code: "not_found", message: "."}`` for missing ref OR
cross-tenant ref, both return the same 404 body to prevent
enumeration of blobs belonging to other organisations.

Scope: requires `` scope.
get.summary: Fetch stored screenshot blob by UUID ref

post.description:
multi-backend search.

Engine selection: ``engine`` body field or ``X-GhostCrawl-Search-Engine`` header.
Auth: tenant bearer + customer's search API key via
``X-Provider-Authorization: Bearer <key>`` (never persisted).

schema.description: Idempotency key (8–256 ASCII printable chars). Supply a fresh UUID or ULID per logically-distinct request. Replaying the same request with the same key returns the cached response without re-execution. Same key + different body → 409 idempotency_key_reused_with_different_body. Body > 1 MiB with this header → 413.

get.description:
cursor-paginated listing of active sessions.

Requires scope session:read. Sessions created by scrape workers and
via the API are unified in the same listing, all sessions for your
organisation are visible. Results are strictly; no
cross-organisation sessions are returned.

post.description:
explicit session creation with engine preference.

Accepts engine in {chrome, firefox, webkit, auto}.

POST /v1/sessions/{profile_id}/recording/start.

Scope: recordings:write. Tier: interaction (recording_enabled on org).
Returns 201 RecordingResponse; emits recording.started audit.
post.summary: Start an action recording for a session (AGEN)

post.description:
POST /v1/sessions/{profile_id}/recording/stop.

Scope: recordings:write. Tier: interaction.
Idempotent if already finalized, returns 200 with current status.
Emits recording.stopped audit.
post.summary: Stop and finalize an active recording (AGEN)

get.description:
SSE stream of live budget counters for a session.

Emits server-sent events only on counter change (polled every 250 ms).
Heartbeat emitted every 15 seconds to keep the connection alive.
Closes on session end, client disconnect, or server shutdown.

Proxy anti-buffer headers: Cache-Control: no-cache, X-Accel-Buffering: no,
Connection: keep-alive.

Auth: budgets:read for own session. Cross-tenant access returns 404
(not 403) as an enumeration guard, ownership is checked before scope
so that a caller with budgets:read cannot enumerate other organisations'
sessions.

post.description:
extend the session TTL.

Reads session, verifies org ownership,
then bumps the TTL by body.ttl_seconds (or a default extension).

mark the session/identity sticky (pinned).

Reads session, verifies org ownership,
then writes back `pinned=True` + `pinned_at=<epoch>` preserving the TTL.

unpin the session and free the slot.

Reads session, verifies org ownership,
then writes back `pinned=False` + `released_at=<epoch>`.

Action-dispatch shim for the takeover surface (Surface L acceptance test).

POST /v1/sessions/{id}/takeover with {"action": "mint"} delegates to the
HMAC-token mint logic (same as /takeover_token). {"action": "release"}
delegates to the control flag release logic (same as /takeover_release).

This endpoint exists because the Surface L live test uses a single URL
with an action discriminator, while the underlying REST surface exposes
two dedicated sub-paths. Both entry points share identical auth, tier,
and cross-tenant guards.

Release takeover control of the session.

Requires a paid plan.
Returns 200 ``{"released": true}`` on success.
Mint a short-lived session control token for an interactive session.

get.description: owner-scoped cursor pagination.
get.summary: List storage states with cursor pagination

post.description:
snapshot browser storage state from a live session.

Captures the current cookies, localStorage, and session storage from the
named active profile and saves it encrypted under the given name.
Returns storage state metadata on success (credentials are never returned).
Requires scope storage:write.
Storage blobs over 5 MB return storage_state_too_large (400).
post.summary: Save storage state from live session

post.description:
clear profile sticky-attach binding.

Removes the storage state binding from a profile so subsequent sessions
start without a pre-loaded storage state. Returns 204.

delete.description:
cascade binding via.

Returns 204 on success, 404 (storage_state_not_found) on miss.
Emits storage_state.deleted audit.

get.description:
metadata only.

Cross-tenant miss returns storage_state_not_found (404, not 403; enumeration guard).

post.description:
attach a storage state to a profile.

Decrypts and validates the named storage state, filters expired cookies,
then sets the sticky binding so subsequent sessions on this profile start
with the saved state loaded.

Cross-tenant miss → storage_state_not_found (404, not 403; enumeration guard).
Corrupted data → storage_state_decrypt_failed (400).

get.description:
Check if a newer release tag is available.
Returns: JSON with ``newer_available``, ``newer_tag``, ``release_notes_url``.

schema.description: Currently installed tag, e.g. v1. 8. 0
schema.title: Current

get.description:
cursor pagination.

Scope: webhooks:read. cross-tenant → 200 + empty items (store filters by org_id). tier gate NOT applied (already-registered webhooks always listable). owner_id filter requires webhooks:admin; without it → 403.

post.description: Create a new webhook subscription. Returns the webhook plus the plaintext signing secret once. Store the secret immediately, it cannot be recovered; only rotation via /rotate-secret provides a new one.

delete.description:
DELETE /v1/webhooks/{webhook_id}.

Scope: webhooks:write. Tier gate: NOT applied (asymmetry).
Cross-tenant → 404. secret keys deleted best-effort.

get.description:
404 byte-identical for missing AND cross-tenant.

Scope: webhooks:read. Tier gate: NOT applied.
Cross-tenant → 404 (by-id asymmetry). Secret: NOT returned (/10).

GET /v1/webhooks/{webhook_id}/deliveries.

Scope: webhooks:read. Tier gate: NOT applied.
Cross-tenant → 404 (by-id asymmetry).

post.description:
POST /v1/webhooks/{webhook_id}/deliveries/{delivery_id}/retry.

Scope: webhooks:write. No tier gate. Cross-tenant → 404.

Re-enqueues the failed delivery with a fresh delivery_id; the original
event_id is preserved so downstream consumers can detect the retry.

POST /v1/webhooks/{webhook_id}/rotate-secret.

Generate a new signing secret for the webhook. The previous secret remains valid
for a short grace period so in-flight deliveries can still be verified.
post.summary: Rotate webhook signing secret (previous secret valid during grace period).
